frameworks.name: National Guidelines on AI Governance and Ethics
frameworks.description: Malaysia's national AI governance framework establishing 7 ethical principles for AI development and deployment. Theme: 'AI for Malaysia, AI for All'. Adapted from UNESCO, OECD, and European Commission standards.

frameworks.name: Artificial Intelligence Act
frameworks.description: World's first comprehensive AI law. Risk-based approach with prohibited, high-risk, limited-risk, and minimal-risk categories. Penalties up to 35M EUR or 7% of global turnover.

frameworks.name: AI Risk Management Framework 1.0
frameworks.description: Operational framework for managing AI risks across the AI lifecycle. Organized into 4 functions (Govern, Map, Measure, Manage) with categories and subcategories.

frameworks.name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — AI Management System
frameworks.description: First international certifiable AI management system standard. Follows Annex SL structure (clauses 4-10) with Annex A controls and Annex B guidance. Content is constructed-indicative (paywalled standard).

frameworks.name: Model AI Governance Framework
frameworks.description: Practical governance framework with Agentic AI addendum (2025). Organized around internal governance, risk management, operations management, and stakeholder interaction.

frameworks.name: OECD Recommendation on Artificial Intelligence
frameworks.description: First intergovernmental AI principles. 5 values-based principles + 5 recommendations for policy. Updated in 2024 to reflect generative AI developments. Adopted by 46 countries.

frameworks.name: Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
frameworks.description: First global normative instrument on AI ethics. 4 core values, 10 principles, and 11 policy action areas. Adopted by 193 member states.

frameworks.name: Interim Measures for the Management of Generative AI Services
frameworks.description: China's binding regulations for generative AI services. Covers content safety, data labeling, algorithmic transparency, and user protection. Part of a broader AI regulatory ecosystem.

frameworks.name: A Pro-Innovation Approach to AI Regulation
frameworks.description: UK's principles-based approach relying on existing regulators. 5 cross-sectoral principles applied through sector-specific regulators rather than a single AI law.

frameworks.name: Executive Order on Artificial Intelligence
frameworks.description: EO 14179 (Jan 2025) replacing Biden-era EO 14110. Focuses on removing barriers to AI leadership, promoting innovation, and maintaining US competitiveness. Revoked prior safety-focused requirements.

frameworks.name: Artificial Intelligence and Data Act
frameworks.description: Part of Bill C-27 (Digital Charter Implementation Act). Includes AIDA (proposed, died on order paper) and the Voluntary Code of Conduct on the Responsible Development and Management of Advanced Generative AI Systems.
